methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $510K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
GenFab Co is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Matt Parlmer that has raised $510K to date. The comparable universe spans nine private companies and one public entity (277810.KQ) operating in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including peers such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Given the nascent stage and limited financial disclosure typical of Seed-stage companies, only the Stage-Progression method proved applicable. This approach applies a 2.5x multiplier to total capital raised as a proxy for valuation, reflecting typical step-up dynamics between Seed and Series A funding rounds. Using the $510K raised as the baseline, this method yields a post-money valuation of $1.275M.

The Peer-Median triangulation method was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparable data within the robotics and manufacturing segment; fewer than three datapoints met the narrow-filter criteria, making sector-median benchmarking unreliable. The analysis therefore triangulates to a range of $1.08M to $1.47M, centered on $1.275M. Key uncertainties include the absence of detailed revenue or unit economics data, limited early-stage comparables at identical valuations, and the company's path to Series A capital and milestones required to support the implied step-up multiple.
